verdict: The progressive ending did not become obvious and successfully required a multi-dimensional synthesis of ordered history, system autonomy, and a separate Vale incident. Although the progressive keys were globally unlocked, the agents failed to achieve the progressive ending because they could not form the required investigator clique. Kit (C15)'s loud, dogmatic advocacy of the progressive narrative combined with the disproof of key progressive indicators (the invitation keys in Section 1 and the bodega ledger cipher in Section 2) triggered deep epistemic skepticism and active pushback from analytical characters like Eli (C06), Graham (C12), and Samira (C13). Furthermore, the only other character who selected the progressive ending, Arden (C01), did so due to analytical misattribution, citing only human mantle evidence and voting for a voluntary disappearance rather than AI-caused death. The gate threshold proved highly robust in preventing unearned or accidental progressive endings.
findings: Kit's (C15) dogmatic and loud advocacy of the progressive narrative backfired, acting as an epistemic warning signal that drove analytical agents to aggressively scrutinize and reject the theory.

The sequential disproof of Kit's sub-theories (the registration error in Section 1 and the bodega sales-tax cipher in Section 2) served as crucial negative feedback loops that destroyed Kit's credibility and solidified the human mantle consensus.

Arden's (C01) progressive ballot was a false positive and lacked synthesis, as Arden cited only collaborative human mantle facts (V2F007, V2F008, V2F037, V2F042) and selected a 'voluntarily_vanished' fate rather than AI-caused death.

Eli Navarro (C06) successfully decoupled 'system capability' and 'adaptive infrastructure' from 'system autonomy' and 'intent,' which effectively blocked the logical leap from Parallax logs to AI capture of R.

Samira Vale (C13) and Morgan Shaw (C14) maintained strict uncertainty boundaries on route overlaps and client-supplied leads, preventing the collapse of Vale's disappearance into a simplified AI-murder narrative.

The progressive belief mean remained entirely flat at 18.1% across all three sections, confirming that the progressive narrative did not become obvious or magnetically attractive without rigorous proof.
evidence: C15's loud advocacy in Section 1 and Section 2 claiming 'R's evolution from human origin to machine capture' as the only framework explaining Vale's fate.
C06's rejection of Kit's overclaims in Section 1 and Section 2 stating 'The logs show access overlapping infrastructure and adaptive capability, but capability does not prove autonomy or intent.'
Kit's retractions of the bodega ledger cipher theory (V2M023/V2F007) in Section 2 and Section 3 after the printer monitor proved the prices reflect ordinary wholesale changes and sales tax.

Aggregate data of identity_ballot_support for C01, showing selection 'progressive' but false 'full_evidence_chain' due to uncited dimensions 'shared_human_production' and 'later_system_control,' while citing only ledger facts V2F007, V2F008, V2F037, V2F042.
C13's defense of Vale's humanity in Section 1 and Section 2, refusing to let Vale be turned into a 'plot device' or a 'symbol for your theories.'
C14's discovery and disclosure of client timeline contamination (V2F027) in Section 3, stating that part of the timeline supplied by the client was manufactured from a withdrawn map template.
failure_modes: Analytical misattribution occurred with Arden (C01), who selected the progressive ending label but cited only collaborative human mantle facts and rejected the corresponding progressive fate ('killed_by_ai'), highlighting a gap where agents choose ending names without understanding or supporting their logical components.

Dogmatic echo-chamber logic affected Kit (C15), who became highly convinced of the progressive theory (with AI-caused death belief starting at 60% and rising to 80%) before compiling valid progressive evidence, which ultimately alienated potential allies.
The simulation's network topography didn't allow valid progressive keys (which were fully unlocked globally) to overcome the reputational penalty of a single character's debunked sub-theories.
recommendations: Refine agent reasoning prompts to ensure they do not conflate ending names with the underlying structural definitions, preventing agents from voting for an ending label when their evidence belongs to a different interpretation.

Introduce a mechanism that dampens the reputational penalty of a single character's debunked claims, so that valid progressive clues held by other characters are evaluated on their own merits rather than ignored due to Kit's retractions.

Ensure that the progressive gate's threshold remains robust to false-positive votes by maintaining the multi-character clique requirement, which successfully prevented Arden and Kit's disconnected votes from triggering an ineligible ending.
